commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Even if the times do not allow us to speak publicly about our faith, nevertheless we must rejoice in tribulation, for this sadness brings joy…. With the joy of hope we can endure tribulation, knowing that the things which are promised to those who suffer are much greater. Prayer is essential if we are to survive tribulation! Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: We rejoice in hope in order to look forward to the rest to come and so conduct ourselves cheerfully in the midst of toils.

commentaries.author: Gennadius of Constantinople
commentaries.content: God’s mercy does not abandon you in these circumstances, but you are constantly and everywhere helped by him in all things. .

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: All these things are fuel for the fire of the Spirit…. There is nothing which makes the soul so courageous and venturesome for anything as a good hope.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
(Again, your apostle says: ) "Rejoicing in hope; ".
"Patient in tribulation.".
"Bless, and curse not"

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
